finding.nomination: NAB2 is a Th1/Th2 program regulator
finding.disease_link: atopic eczema (Open Targets GWAS-genetic; flagged as genetic, not experimentally re-derived here)
finding.lbd_universe: 30 clean full-chain-supported questions out of 3,935 candidate A-genes

HOP-0.name: KNOCKDOWN-QC GATE
HOP-0.role: hero gate — only a silenced gene yields an interpretable downstream result; a failed knockdown is UNTESTED, never 'no effect'

cis_artifact_check.name: CIS-ARTIFACT falsification (Stage 3, native S3 read)
cis_artifact_check.hypothesis_tested: NAB2 guide is ~1.9 kb from STAT6 (master Th2 regulator); could the eczema signal be STAT6 bleed?
cis_artifact_check.conclusion: CRISPRi cis-artifact REFUTED; the eczema effect is genuinely NAB2's, not STAT6 bleed

what_ran_where.component: LBD generator over full 3,935-gene Stim8hr universe
what_ran_where.execution: native, deterministic code path replaying cached Europe PMC / Open Targets JSON receipts from workbench-local cache; Stage-0 probe separately proved live Europe PMC GET + Open Targets GraphQL POST
native pandas over the deposited tables; funnel and NAB2 rank reproduced digit-for-digit
live read of authors' deposited genome-wide DE matrix from public S3

cross_model_replication: kept external as an independent auditor (a second model family)
overall_verdict: CONSISTENT WITH a validated gene -> Th1/Th2 program -> disease chain, re-derived from the tables: a novel, reproducible NOMINATION that NAB2 is a Th1/Th2 regulator; the atopic-eczema disease link is Open-Targets GWAS-genetic and flagged as such.
